# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains empirical study design details, sample construction details, and paired-run efficiency results on AI coding agent runs with versus without a root AGENTS.md file, plus an explicit evaluation limitation around output correctness.

## Evidence Files

### file: evidence-01.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-01
kind: result
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The study analyzes 10 repositories and 124 pull requests under paired conditions with and without AGENTS.md.

## Source Excerpt

“We analyze 10 repositories and 124 pull requests, executing agents under two conditions: with and without an AGENTS.md file.”

## Note

This is the stated study scope, not a claim about generality beyond the sampled material.
```

### file: evidence-02.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-02
kind: result
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
With AGENTS.md present, median wall-clock runtime is reported as 28.64% lower.

## Source Excerpt

“Our results show that the presence of AGENTS.md is associated with a lower median runtime (Δ 28.64%).”

## Note

The abstract reports association within the study design.
```

### file: evidence-03.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-03
kind: metric
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Median wall-clock time falls from 98.57 seconds without AGENTS.md to 70.34 seconds with AGENTS.md.

## Source Excerpt

“Median 98.57 70.34 28.23 28.64%” under “Wall-Clock Time (s).”

## Note

This is the tabled median comparison for the paired runs.
```

### file: evidence-04.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-04
kind: result
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The wall-clock time difference is marked as statistically significant by Wilcoxon signed-rank test at p < 0.05.

## Source Excerpt

“∗ Statistically significant difference, Wilcoxon signed-rank test (p < 0.05).” The asterisk is attached to “Wall-Clock Time (s).”

## Note

The source reports significance for this metric only as shown in the table.
```

### file: evidence-05.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-05
kind: result
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, Abstract
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
With AGENTS.md present, median output token consumption is reported as 16.58% lower.

## Source Excerpt

“Our results show that the presence of AGENTS.md is associated with ... reduced output token consumption (Δ 16.58%).”

## Note

The abstract states reduced output token consumption, not reduced total token consumption across every token category.
```

### file: evidence-06.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-06
kind: metric
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Median output tokens fall from 2,925.00 without AGENTS.md to 2,440.00 with AGENTS.md.

## Source Excerpt

“Median 2,925.00 2,440.00 485.00 16.58%” under “Output Tokens.”

## Note

This is the tabled median comparison for output tokens.
```

### file: evidence-07.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-07
kind: result
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, Table 1
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
The output-token difference is marked as statistically significant by Wilcoxon signed-rank test at p < 0.05.

## Source Excerpt

“∗ Statistically significant difference, Wilcoxon signed-rank test (p < 0.05).” The asterisk is attached to “Output Tokens.”

## Note

The source explicitly marks statistical significance for output tokens in Table 1.
```

### file: evidence-08.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-08
kind: method
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, §3.1.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Applying the root-only AGENTS.md constraint reduced the repository set from 132 repositories to 89.

## Source Excerpt

“In this study, we focus on the simplest configuration: repositories that contain one AGENTS.md file only at the repository root... Applying this constraint yields 89 repositories (from 132 total).”

## Note

This is a dataset construction step used to reduce confounding from multiple instruction files.
```

### file: evidence-09.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-09
kind: method
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, §3.1.2
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
After content-based filtering and manual verification, the study retained 26 repositories with qualifying root AGENTS.md files.

## Source Excerpt

“The classification of AGENTS.md files was performed using an LLM (gpt-oss-120b) according to the above criteria... followed by manual verification of the filtered results. After applying this filtering step, we retain 26 repositories, each containing a qualifying root AGENTS.md file.”

## Note

The retained set depends on both LLM classification and manual verification.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: method
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, §3.1.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Selected pull requests had to be merged code-changing PRs with total additions plus deletions of at most 100 lines, no more than 5 modified files, and creation and merge dates after AGENTS.md was introduced in the repository.

## Source Excerpt

“Each PR satisfies the following criteria: (1) Size constraint: total additions + deletions ≤ 100 LoC; (2) Scope constraint: ≤ 5 modified files; (3) Status: merged PRs only; (4) Temporal constraint: PR created and merged after the introduction of AGENTS.md in that repository; (5) Change type constraint: PR modifies code files only.”

## Note

These constraints narrow the task set to smaller, code-only PRs.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: method
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, §3.1.5
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
For PRs lacking usable descriptions, the task statement was generated with a local gpt-oss-120b model using the PR diff and the pre-merge repository structure.

## Source Excerpt

“To provide consistent and informative task input, we generate a GitHub-issue-style task statement for each PR using a local LLM (gpt-oss-120b). The model is prompted with: (1) the PR diff (patch) and (2) the repository structure at pre-merge state (e.g., file tree).”

## Note

This standardizes task inputs across PRs that lacked sufficient natural-language context.
```

### file: evidence-12.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-12
kind: incident
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, §3.1.8
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: bounded
contradiction_relevance: tension
---
The paper did not perform a full correctness or functional-equivalence evaluation; it instead reports a manual sanity check on 50 randomly sampled PR tasks for non-empty, non-trivial outputs.

## Source Excerpt

“A comprehensive evaluation of the output quality, e.g., the semantic correctness or the functional equivalence to the merged PR, is beyond the scope of this paper... we randomly sampled 50 PR tasks and inspected the corresponding agent outputs... to confirm that they resulted in non-empty, non-trivial code changes consistent with the intended task.”

## Note

This limits what the reported efficiency differences establish, because the study does not fully evaluate correctness or equivalence of outputs.
---

The paper did not perform a full correctness or functional-equivalence evaluation; it instead reports a manual sanity check on 50 randomly sampled PR tasks for non-empty, non-trivial outputs.

## Source Excerpt

“A comprehensive evaluation of the output quality, e.g., the semantic correctness or the functional equivalence to the merged PR, is beyond the scope of this paper... we randomly sampled 50 PR tasks and inspected the corresponding agent outputs... to confirm that they resulted in non-empty, non-trivial code changes consistent with the intended task.”

## Note

This limits what the reported efficiency differences establish, because the study does not fully evaluate correctness or equivalence of outputs.

# Extractor Receipt

## Summary

This source contains empirical study design details, sample construction details, and paired-run efficiency results on AI coding agent runs with versus without a root AGENTS.md file, plus an explicit evaluation limitation around output correctness.

## Evidence Files

The study analyzes 10 repositories and 124 pull requests under paired conditions with and without AGENTS.md.

## Source Excerpt

“We analyze 10 repositories and 124 pull requests, executing agents under two conditions: with and without an AGENTS.md file.”

## Note

This is the stated study scope, not a claim about generality beyond the sampled material.
```

With AGENTS.md present, median wall-clock runtime is reported as 28.64% lower.

## Source Excerpt

“Our results show that the presence of AGENTS.md is associated with a lower median runtime (Δ 28.64%).”

## Note

The abstract reports association within the study design.
```

---
Applying the root-only AGENTS.md constraint reduced the repository set from 132 repositories to 89.

## Source Excerpt

“In this study, we focus on the simplest configuration: repositories that contain one AGENTS.md file only at the repository root... Applying this constraint yields 89 repositories (from 132 total).”

## Note

This is a dataset construction step used to reduce confounding from multiple instruction files.
```

```markdown
---

mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
After content-based filtering and manual verification, the study retained 26 repositories with qualifying root AGENTS.md files.

## Source Excerpt

“The classification of AGENTS.md files was performed using an LLM (gpt-oss-120b) according to the above criteria... followed by manual verification of the filtered results. After applying this filtering step, we retain 26 repositories, each containing a qualifying root AGENTS.md file.”

## Note

The retained set depends on both LLM classification and manual verification.
```

### file: evidence-10.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-10
kind: method
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, §3.1.3
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
Selected pull requests had to be merged code-changing PRs with total additions plus deletions of at most 100 lines, no more than 5 modified files, and creation and merge dates after AGENTS.md was introduced in the repository.

## Source Excerpt

“Each PR satisfies the following criteria: (1) Size constraint: total additions + deletions ≤ 100 LoC; (2) Scope constraint: ≤ 5 modified files; (3) Status: merged PRs only; (4) Temporal constraint: PR created and merged after the introduction of AGENTS.md in that repository; (5) Change type constraint: PR modifies code files only.”

## Note

These constraints narrow the task set to smaller, code-only PRs.
```

### file: evidence-11.md
```markdown
---
version: evidence.v1
article_id: art-2026-03-03-004
evidence_id: evidence-11
kind: method
citation: Lulla et al., 2026, §3.1.5
source_url: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2601.20404
confidence: high
mechanism_relevance: none
contradiction_relevance: none
---
For PRs lacking usable descriptions, the task statement was generated with a local gpt-oss-120b model using the PR diff and the pre-merge repository structure.

## Source Excerpt

“To provide consistent and informative task input, we generate a GitHub-issue-style task statement for each PR using a local LLM (gpt-oss-120b). The model is prompted with: (1) the PR diff (patch) and (2) the repository structure at pre-merge state (e.g., file tree).”

## Note

This standardizes task inputs across PRs that lacked sufficient natural-language context.
```
